text: Aurejin Saves Jessalyn
11 ABY
Jessalyn Valios
Yavin 4
Jessalyn Valios, Aurejin
Jedi, Sith
Forthcoming.

Jessalyn%rAn intimidating figure dressed entirely in black. Obviously female and%rhuman, her tall, slender body is covered with a form-fitting black%routfit made of an iridescent material. One of her legs is swathed in%rbandages that hold bacta patches in place, and she has obvious%rdifficulty with mobility.. Her porcelain face is scarred with a fresh%rwound that gouges its way from her right temple to her chin, badly%rmarring one eye; but the other smolders with a green, fiery intensity.%rAround her slim waist is a plain black belt fastened with a polished%rsilver buckle, and a black cloak, somewhat tattered, is thrown back from%rher shoulders, falling just to her knees when it's not fluttering in the%rbreeze.

Aurejin%rIf one remembers people's faces by recalling specific features,%rindividual aspects of them that seem striking, then Aurejin is composed%rof: thick, dark hair, dark eyes, smooth, pale skin with no facial hair,%ra symmetry and composition about the head that is regarded as handsome%rby most. He's a bit too tall, too lithe and narrow for an average human,%rand is probably some closely related species. All in all, a collection%rof unremarkable features. However, when he is taken as a whole, there is%rmore to him: a graceful, almost elegant poise in his step, a clear and%rdeliberate element in his voice, history on his face and an awareness in%rhis eye which suggests that he is *about* something larger than that in%rwhich he is currently engaged.%rA plain, neutral-colored shirt hangs on his shoulders, so large as to%rresemble a poncho except for the sides being mostly closed. The sleeves%rare so wide as to be billowing with the front of the garment completely%rflat and unadorned. Drab. The collar of the shirt is laced with some%rloose hanging cord, sagging laces in X shapes but set up to allow for%rcomfort and mobility and revealing plenty of the tight black shirt%runderneath. A long loop of thin silver chain hangs from his right side,%rits beginning concealed somewhere beneath his shirt. His gray breeches%rare similarly loose and well-worn. A pair of ankle-high black boots with%rlarge treaded soles complete the outfit.
Ravine -- Jungle Wilderness

Totally surrounded on all sides by the rich vegetation of the%rforest, this ravine is eerily silent. The canopy overhead blocks out all%rlight from above. The floor here is composed of thick black dirt and a%rfew gangly vines which sprout up from the ground. Immense walls of%rvegetation enclose the ravine from two sides, while a huge log has%rfallen across the south edge of the gully, blocking all passage in that%rdirection. To the southwest, and encrusted in vines is a towering%rZiggurat. An enormous gaping door in it's side sitting like a massive%rdarkened cave. A few lights around the exterior tell you that it is%roccupied.

There has been a lot of activity here lately. A newly trod path winds%rthrough the thick jungle underbrush toward the nearby temple, the group%rof six small but determined but meeting with only mixed success once%rthey had gained access. Roth had been something of a puzzle, though it%rwas becoming more complete in Aurejin's head; he would be dangerous to%rdeal with, though necessary. It was all Aurejin could do to keep from%rresorting to all-out battle back there. Fighting had not been the plan.%rIt was better to test the boy's limits to begin with, and lay plans from%rthere.

The rest of the group had wandered out cautiously sometime after%rAurejin, the tall Jedi's head full of his own abstract thoughts. So much%rto work out in such a short amount of time. The others were around%rsomewhere, though Aurejin didn't particularly feel the need to watch%rover them. He knew Roth was gone and wouldn't be coming back in the%rimmediate future. Better to let them find Simon and return to the ship%rby themselves so he could think. And so he does, wandering roughly along%rthe line of their old path, stepping lightly, touching his hand on a%rstump or branch every now and then for support.
Aurejin pauses, and squints, staring up at the canopy far overhead.%rSomething...

He may have seen the flash of the starfighter that appeared for a moment%rin Yavin's skies, and then disappeared, swallowed up by the canopy. Or%rperhaps he sensed the indelible imprint in the Force that precedes the%rambiguous and certainly dangerous fallen Jedi who has been stalking them%rsince their departure from Caspar. Jessalyn's approach is quiet and%rstealthful, though her altered aura is projected before her, coming from%rno one clear direction. A biting cold incongruous with the muggy heat of%rYavin's jungle envelopes Aurejin, and her laughter is felt more than%rheard, like a witch's cackle... proud, powerful, and... resigned.
_Aurejin... Jedi of the Old Ways... I have found you so that you can%rcomplete your purpose._

A slow chill slides up Aurejin's spine, raising those tiny hairs along%rthe back of his neck despite the oppressive humidity. He turns and looks%rbehind him, his once-honed senses still precise and full of power though%runpracticed. "But what do you know of my purpose, my lost friend?" he%rcalls out, projected more up than any other direction in lieu of precise%rinformation of where she was lurking. The man's voice is amazingly loud%rwith a very small amount of effort. All in all, it's like an invisible%rpublic address system is following him around, his rich voice rolling%roff into the woods in every direction. "Once you knew, perhaps."

Finally a rustling sound starts from the very top of the ravine, though%rits source is hidden by the vegetation that surrounds the entire area so%rthickly. Only when she is some twenty meters from the ground does%rJessalyn appear, clinging to a thick, rope vine which she nimbly%rdescends. When her feet fall upon the jungle floor, she turns slowly,%rfacing Aurejin as she pushes her cloak back over her shoulders and it's%rlifted by a hot gust of wind.

"I did, once," she replies, her voice almost human again, her green eyes%rfierce and sad all at once. "In a way, I still do. I know that your%rintent is to destroy me. I can only hope that you will be successful."

Aurejin turns to face her, letting his arms hang in front of his body,%rwrists coming together and rolling, hands wrapping together elegantly.%rHe sizes her up for a long time, unblinking gaze cutting away the%rsuperfluous. Finally, he speaks. "You're mistaken." To that end, he%rturns to the side and exhales, glad to be had that settled. The tall man%rsits down wearily, the soft remnants of a fallen tree serving as a%rconvenient moss-covered perch. "I'm hardly interested in destroying%ranyone, though my group..." The Jedi waves a hand toward the temple.%r"Would likely prefer me be their personal assassin that what I am. No...%rI don't want to destroy you. I want to uncover you. To clear away your%rshroud." He places a hand on each knee and stares at the ground in front%rof his feet, sounding tired. "But it seems as difficult to separate from%ryou as Mira's crown is to her."

With a quiet chuckle, Jessalyn watches Aurejin take a seat on the mossy%rlog, and she steps slowly forward, boots crunching in the underbrush%rbefore she reaches the newly-made path. "Of course... you -are- a Jedi.%rYour way is not destruction... but redemption." She seems both irritated%rand comforted by this fact, and gives Aurejin a level look, gauging him%ras deeply as he had inspected her. When she speaks again her voice is%rbarely above a whisper. "I fear there is no hope for me. When I cannot%reven choose my own destiny..." She shakes her head, the words trailing%roff to dissolve into the sounds of the jungle, and she closes her eyes.%r"The only choice I have is death."

Aurejin's eyebrow cocks and he looks at Jessalyn with a loaded%rexpression. "Your thoughts betray you," he says, standing quickly and%rstepping toward her. "Your willingness to end it all is your true self%rspeaking. Do you understand?" The tall man's brow grows dark and he%rstalks toward her, leaning forward and closing the space between them to%rbut a meter. "While it's there, while the Jedi that *was* is there,%rfinish it, for all our good." He levels a hand and a long, rigid index%rfinger at her cloak, to the semi-concealed blaster at her belt. "Finish%rit!" he bellows. The jungle around them erupts with hoots and calls,%ravian creatures exploding from tree tops.

She nearly cringes at the volume of Aurejin's commanding voice, and%rtakes a step backward from him, the conflict clear upon her face as well%ras the churning of emotions that roil within Jessalyn's heart. "I -- I%rcan't!" she cries. "He forbade it!" And yet, one black-gloved hand%rtrembles, moving to the blaster at her side and pulling it from its%rholster by slow, agonizing degrees.

Sweat beads up and then rolls down Jessalyn's forehead, stinging her%reyes as she gazes at the other Jedi -- lost, alone, but determined. _It%rwasn't supposed to end this way._ She lets her breath out in a gasp as%rshe stares into the barrel of the blaster, fighting off layers of deceit%rand control that have bound her for so long, aching for the one willful%raction that could finally end this living nightmare. "Tell Luke... how%rsorry I am," she whispers, eyes closing as her finger depresses the%rtrigger.

It was a gamble that would have had an extremely traumatic impact upon%rthe woman regardless of how it turned out. But it was a high-stakes game%rlong before this moment, and... well, Aurejin decides as she reaches for%rthe blaster, that she would know peace no matter how it ends. As she%rpulls the weapon and aims it, he moves forward and sidesteps. Full of%rthe Force, an instrument of it, the Jedi's own will is gone. His saber%ris suddenly there, hacking downwards into the few centimeters of space%rbetween the woman's head and the end of her weapon. The wall of%rprotective violet is nothing less than perfectly timed, the killing bolt%rspattering harmlessly up into the trees. Aurejin's weapon reverses%rcourse in the same amount of time, collapsing as he clips it back on his%rbelt. All in less than a second. Both hands now free, the Aurejin puts a%rhand on her wrist to point the blaster at the earth and eases a%rcomforting arm over her shoulders. "Your journey begins anew, my%rfriend," he whispers. "Hope... has won the day." A curled tuft of red%rhair floats gently to the forest floor.

Shaking, and utterly prepared for death for yet another time, Jessalyn%ris stunned by Aurejin's action. Before she can even blink, the violet%rblade has passed before her eyes, protecting her from the deadly laser%rbolt. The woman doesn't know whether to be relieved and grateful or%rlivid. She stares at Aurejin as he lowers her hand, and her suddenly%rnumb fingers let go of the blaster so that it falls into the dirt.

Trying to speak, Jessalyn's lips move a few times before she leans%rwearily against the supporting arm, watching the tendril of hair drift%rlike a feather to fall beside the forsaken blaster. "I don't know how I%rdid it," she admits, turning watery eyes full of gratitude on this%rstranger who was not a stranger.

Walks cleared her head. Always had it been that way, always would it be%rthat way. And despite risk and danger, mailyn always felt that these%rwalks were better taken alone. Sometimes one needed the company of just%rthoughts. So many things niggled in her mind. Things about Mira, things%rabout Aurejin, things about Simon. Even things about Jessalyn. It didn't%rmake sense, and yet it did. The conflicting emotions are a roiling sea.%rShe must keep her focus. That focus was Mira and the evil contraption%rstuck on her head. She hears voices, and some other odd noises. What she%rsees stuns her. Jessalyn. How did she get here? Mailyn had checked her%rhoming device. And Why did Aurejin have his arms around her. What%rdeception was this?

"Strength," Aurejin says, supplying the answer and patting Jessalyn's%rshoulder softly. "You'll need far more, I fear, for what lies ahead."%rNightmares, doubt, lingering darkness, and guilt. "But for now, rest and%rmourn your passing." To bury it here would be good, to let Valios mark%rthis moment as a milestone, to make a clean break with what she had been%rtwisted into and know that at least one person believed in her. Still...%rAurejin had this sort of faith in all people. It wasn't just Jessalyn,%rbut his obligation to her was more profound. And it was selfish: the%rcoming days and battles would be all but impossible for him alone.%rQuietly, he just pats her on the shoulder repeatedly and lets her look%ron this place with new eyes and wonder.

Mailyn. If it wasn't for careful training and manners, Aurejin would%rprobably curse. He does scowl. Her influence here could be damaging. He%rgives the approaching woman a welcoming nod to acknowledge her presence,%rsilently preparing himself to make Mailyn a casualty of the war between%rthe Dark and the Light. Jessalyn's redemption was that important.

Still trembling, the tears leak from Jessalyn's eyes like a child%rawakened from the worst kind of nightmare, frightened by the shadows and%rlingering memories, but relieved beyond measure that it is ended. At%rleast the worst of it, she tells herself, aware still of that part of%rher mind invaded and manipulated by Palpatine, but held at bay by a%rstrength of spirit and a belief in herself that Jessalyn never was%rcapable of before. Questions and torments tumble in her mind, and she%rwants to speak about them, pour out all her reservations to this comrade%rwho had helped her when no one else could. "Thank you," is all she%rmanages to say, before her attention is diverted to the newcomer, and%rJessa turns her head to gaze sadly at Mailyn, her body tensing like a%rcoiled spring at the sight of her.
Aurejin sends through the Force...%rPeace. Trust will be difficult for them.

All Mailyn sees is the scowl. All she senses is hatred. All she feels is%rbetrayal. She does not understand. Aurejin, Ionnous, whoever he was -%rnothing she knew was the truth. About any of it. She knew what she had%rseen, and what she had felt. What she had heard and what she had been%rtold. But it did not make sense. Suddenly helping Mira does not seem to%rmatter anymore. It was bigger than she was and beyond the scope of what%rshe was capable of. She had risked so much for nothing. Good, evil it%rdid not matter any more. It was Jessica Marama all over again. Same game%rwith different players. And once again she was nobody's priority. Not%rthat she ever had been, or expected to be. She was just an intruder. She%rshakes her head and just begins to back away, not caring where she went.%rShe'd find her own way off this jungle world. She'd no more be a part of%rthis.

Aurejin realizes he's been holding his breath extremely steady. His%rchest deflates slightly as Mailyn begins to retreat. "Raines," he calls%rout, stepping forward tentatively with his arm still on Jessalyn. "Wait.%rDo you remember?" He gives a small hop and balances atop the other end%rof the fallen tree that had been a seat for him only a moment ago. The%rtall Jedi cranes his neck to find her in the thick growth. "Do you%rremember what I said? About having to trust me? It's difficult, I know.%rBut you must." One arm is extended out straight toward Jessalyn, like a%rbalance, though in fact his hand is outstretched toward the woman, as if%rto make sure he doesn't lose the delicate Jedi. For Aurejin, what Mailyn%rthought about him was less important than their fragile alliance. The%rtall man was about something else entirely, his attention on more than%rjust making friends.
But still. Aurejin does care, and a wry, sheepish smile lifts one side%rof his face as he looks down toward Jessalyn. "I've done nothing. It was%ryou, every moment."

Mira wanders out of the forest, where she had found a small stream. This%rwas handy, not only b/c it allowed her to sail various sticks and small%rbranches down stream like boats for amusement, but also because it%rallowed her to wash off the insect-attracting balm from her neck. The%r(now devoid of insect carcass) net is still draped over her head,%rslightly damp from the washing it got in the water.

As she emerges into the clearing, she comes to a halt, staring between%rJessalyn and Mailyn and Aurejin. What was going on? "Hello..." she says%rtrepidatiously, ready to run in the event that Jessalyn tried to kill%rher.

The tentative smile on Jessalyn's face appears despite her misgivings,%rand she wipes at her face with the back of her hand. She gives Aurejin a%rsuccinct but knowing nod of her head, then looks toward Mailyn, relaxing%rsomewhat, and wondering if she still looks as much like the vile demon%rshe knew she had become. She swallows.

"Mailyn," she calls softly. "Don't go... there's nothing to fear... I%r--" Jessalyn gasps as Mira appears, and her fingers dig involuntarily%rinto Aurejin's arm. "I can give no excuse that would assuage your fears.%rBut I will not harm you... any of you..." Her pleading look turns to%rMira, full of sorrow for what the younger girl has been through, and%rgently wrapping her up with the warm emotions and care that she's always%rfelt for the odd little apprentice. "Please."
You sent through the Force to Aurejin...%rI can't blame anyone for never trusting me again...
Aurejin sends through the Force...%rOurs is a calling that is hard for them to understand.

For a moment Mailyn pauses. But she does not understand, and she finds%rthat she cannot trust anyone or anything. How had she come to this? She%rlooks at Mira with that ridiculous thing on her head and feels sympathy%rfor poor girl, but there is nothing Mailyn can do for her. For any of%rthem. It isn't so much that she feels fear as she feels an overwhelming%rsadness. It threatens to wash over her and steal away any semblance of%rdignity she has left. Amongst this fistful of Jedi she is of little%rconsequence. She had brought them here, and perhaps that was what she%rwas meant to do. She has not the emotional capacity to handle it any%rlonger. She spares Mira an apologetic half smile, but just wordlessly%rshakes her head back and forth, walking backward, letting the depths of%rthe forest swallow her up.

Aurejin steps off of the elevated log with a small shrug. "Raines will%rdo what she thinks is best, of course. We can admire her simple vision,%rat least, admire and respect it, secure in the knowledge that our%ractions are right and hope that she -- and others -- will recognize them%ras so, in time." The tall man lifts a hand to his face and, starting at%rhis brow, runs it roughly down past his chin. It had been a long,%revent-filled day. "Trust... may only be earned, my friends." The%rstatement chills Aurejin, saddens him, that his honored position as a%rmember of that lost Order is not automatically trusted and venerated. It%rwas a different time though, and clinging rigidly to the old ways was%rclearly not possible.

Mira tilts her head to one side as Jessalyn does not throw rocks at her%ror set fire to her head or any of the things Mira had imagined her doing%rwhen she went with Simon on Caspar. In fact, she seems quite like the%rold Jessalyn. "Jessalyn?" Mira queries, taking a few steps closer. As%rshe closes the distance, she can feel, with what little connection with%rthe Force she still retains, that the thing in Jessalyn's mind, the wall%ras she had called it, didn't seem to be there any more. "Hi!" the girl%rcries out, realizing that her friend had been freed from whatever%rPalpatine had done to her, and she rushes over, wrapping Jessalyn up in%ra hug. "Where is Mailyn going?" she asks.

Relief washes through Jessalyn like a cleansing water, and her tears are%rinterrupted with joyous laughter as Mira rushes to her. Returning the%rhug, she holds Mira tightly, protectively, part of her mind already%rdiverted to methods and ideas she might use to free her from the awful%rcontraption Palpatine had attached to her head. "Mira," she whispers,%rheartfelt. The Jedi tilts her head, listening to Aurejin's wise words,%rand she tries to find comfort in what he says. But, she, too, feels the%ranguish of being a Jedi in a universe where Jedi are no longer trusted.%r"I don't think she understands," Jessa replies softly as the woman%rretreats into the shadows of the jungle. "I can't blame her, Mira. I%rdoubt I would trust me if I were in her position." With a sad, wry%rsmile, she nods at Aurejin, repeating his conviction. "Trust can only be%rearned."

"We should probably begin away," Aurejin notes quietly, running a%rfingertip over the spine of a plate-sized leaf that is hanging beside%rhis head. "The walk is a fair distance. Jedi Valios, do you have a ship?%rMarrak and the Selas can take care of Raines." But they would both know%rexactly what he was suggesting: putting Jessalyn on a ship with Mailyn,%rKyyel and Simon would be tantamount to pressing the Big Red Button on%rthe wall of the ship. Self-destruct.
"We need to time to heal, and plan." They needed a retreat, a safe house%rin the midst of all this uncertainty and distrust. They needed a%rSanctuary.

"Simon's going to be mad if I just leave," Mira notes, looking dubiously%rinto the jungle towards the direction Mailyn had disappeared. "He wants%rto train me again," she explains for Jessalyn's benefit. "I showed him%rmy lightsaber. He didn't seem mad when I told him I'd have to think%rabout it. But I don't know if he would like it if I just up and left."

The memory of another Jedi who should have been there flickers across%rJessalyn's mind as she picks up on Aurejin's intent. As she gently pats%rMira's shoulder, the redhead purses her lips thoughtfully. "I have a%rship we can use to get offworld," she replies. "And I know a safe haven%rfor us. I think you especially would appreciate it, Aurejin."

Interrupted by Mira's concerns over Simon, Jessalyn cannot suppress a%rfrown, and she looks between the girl and their new companion without%rspeaking for a moment. "I don't think it would be safe for you to go%rwith him," she says slowly. "Even if it did... make him mad. But of%rcourse, it is your decision. He would train you, but not to be a Jedi.%rHe would train you to destroy us all, Mira." She keeps her hand gently%ron Mira's shoulder, beating back her latent thirst for vengeance. No. It%rwas not something she could give into again. "It's your commitment...%ryour decision."

The tallest of the three simply dips his head courteously at Jessalyn's%rcomment. It was too soon to tell about her, this shaken woman, though%rthe potential advantages of having a group, instead of him alone, were%rbecoming clear already. More resources, more ideas... more tasks could%rbe accomplished. If Mira can be brought around, Aurejin will dare to be%roptimistic, though he says nothing. For now.

Mira pulls away from Jessalyn and sits down on the log, pondering for a%rmoment. "I don't want to train with him so much as I'm afraid of what he%rwill do if I don't," she says, realizing how lame it sounds when she%ractually says it out loud. She shouldn't let fear dictate big decisions%rlike this. "Actually, I'm ready. We can go now," she says, sounding more%rsure of herself. Carefully gathering up the pair fo staffs from the%rground, Mira stands up.

It was decided. Jessalyn breathes out a sigh of relief, sharing her%rsmile with Mira as the girl stands ready to go with them. It would be a%rdifficult road ahead, and she is not yet prepared to consider the%rramifications of what she's done and what damage has been done to her%rspirit.

Still, being free was a start, and she stoops down for a moment to%rretrieve the blaster... and the fallen lock of hair. With a flourish,%rthe weapon is returned to its holster, and she looks at the curling%rred-flamed lock thoughtfully before handing it to Aurejin, smiling%rcrookedly. "You haven't heard of the Refrain of Anshalar, have you?" she%rasks as they begin their trek back to the ship.

With a stiff nod, Aurejin turns to set out along their old trail once%rmore, ready to wade through the rough brush and oppressive humidity.%r"But yet we live," the man murmurs to Mira with a smile, repeating what%rhe had mentioned to her when first the two had met. The girl seemed to%rhave her own sort of appreciation for his nuanced statements, frequently%rarriving at meanings entirely unintended but true enough.

Taking the hair between his fingers since it's offered, the Jedi starts%ralong behind Jessalyn. But his face is suddenly pale, mouth working%rsilently. "Ahn... Anshalar. I've not heard that name in many years...
